page: Amazon Web Services m8azn.6xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9R05 processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 24 dedicated physical cores running at a high clock speed of 5.0 GHz with one thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 96.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage.

Benchmark data highlights the strong computational and memory performance of the m8azn.6xlarge. In single-core processing, the instance achieves top-tier results, as evidenced by stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores in the top 10%. Multi-core performance is also highly competitive, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng and top 10% for Geekbench multi-core. The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers top-tier read and write bandwidth for large, uncached blocks. Cryptographic and vector operations are accelerated by hardware support for AVX-512 and AES instructions, which is reflected in strong Passmark extended instruction and encryption scores.

With a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps and optimized EBS integration, this instance is well-suited for network-intensive and database workloads. It demonstrates top-tier performance in database operations and strong throughput in static web serving and Redis SET operations. Additionally, the high clock speed and memory bandwidth enable efficient execution of lightweight LLM inference tasks. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core allocation and high-frequency CPU provide excellent resource density and predictable performance for production environments, avoiding the multi-threading overhead of shared-core alternatives.
description: The Amazon Web Services m8azn.6xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized instance powered by a 24-core AMD EPYC 9R05 processor running at 5.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and the Nitro hypervisor, it features 24 dedicated physical cores with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio and 96.0 GB of DDR5 memory. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on a 50 Gbps baseline network interface for EBS connectivity. Benchmark results place its single-core and multi-core performance in the top tier, with exceptional memory bandwidth and database processing capabilities. It is highly suited for database operations, web serving, and memory-bound computational workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.6xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring a 24-core AMD EPYC processor at 5.0 GHz, 96 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth for high-performance database and web workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.6xlarge is a 24-core AMD EPYC general-purpose instance with 96 GB DDR5 RAM and 50 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-frequency general-purpose instance featuring dedicated AMD cores, DDR5 memory, and optimized network throughput for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 24-core AMD EPYC 9R05 processor at 5.0 GHz
Equipped with 96.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
Features a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS
Delivers top-tier single-core and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Ideal for database operations, web serving, and lightweight LLM inference
